dashboard.name: 352d8500-b235-4830-a8f6-bb496b3982e7
dashboard.description: Dashboard for Google Threat Intelligence Threat Lists.

query.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = ${entitytype}
$entity_id = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
match:
    $entity_id
outcome:
    $severity=array_distinct(graph.metadata.threat.severity)
    $threat_score = array_distinct(graph.metadata.threat.risk_score)
    $entity_type = array_distinct(graph.metadata.entity_type)
    $threat_list_id=array_distinct(graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"])

dashboardCharts.displayName: IOC URL BY RESPONSE CODE, LAST ANALYSIS

query.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "URL"
graph.entity.url = ${url}

dashboardCharts.displayName: IOC COUNT BY THREAT FEED, BY  DATE

$entity_id = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
match:
    $entity_id
outcome:
    $severity=array_distinct(graph.metadata.threat.severity)
    $threat_score = array_distinct(graph.metadata.threat.risk_score)
    $entity_type = array_distinct(graph.metadata.entity_type)
    $threat_list_id=array_distinct(graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"])

markdown.content:
## DOMAIN IOCS 
---
#### A Domain object encapsulates information pertaining to a specific domain or Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN).

dashboardCharts.name: 21522383-ad8a-454c-88d1-42a84cf1a2f1
dashboardCharts.displayName: MD5 AND FILES BY LAST ANALYSIS TIME

query.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "FILE"
graph.entity.file.md5 = ${md5}

graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "DOMAIN_NAME"
graph.entity.domain.jarm = ${jarm}
$entity_id = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
match:
    $entity_id
outcome:
    $severity=array_distinct(graph.metadata.threat.severity)
    $threat_score = array_distinct(graph.metadata.threat.risk_score)
    $entity_type = array_distinct(graph.metadata.entity_type)
    $threat_list_id=array_distinct(graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"])

markdown.content:
## GTI Categorised Threat Lists  ![alt text](https://files.readme.io/2bd0d6e09e34d1a8075ca7c9fb7a1ff76a11f2636cef55fbcdc89017121f9724-gti-favicon-colored.svg)
---	
#### This dashboard shows GTI Categorised Threat Lists as normalized into the Google SecOps Entity Context Graph (ECG).
 
#### Categorized threat lists are near real-time loc lists that can be used to drive hunting/detection/blocking workflows. They are grouped into categories that can be used to target specific technologies/tech stacks/threats: ransomware, malicious network infrastructure, mobile, OS X, TOP + Trending locs, etc.
---

## FILE IOCS 
---
#### File objects represent a fundamental component of the Google Threat Intelligence (GTI) platform. GTI maintains an extensive repository of over two billion files that have undergone detailed analysis. A file object can be acquired by uploading a new file for analysis, querying for an existing file hash, or through various other search functionalities within the service.

## URL IOCS 
---
#### A `URL` (Uniform Resource Locator) object is distinct from a Domain object in that it specifies the complete path to a particular resource, whereas a domain represents the top-level address (e.g., `www.example.com/page.html` VS. `example.com`). Within Google Threat Intelligence (GTI), `URLs` serve a dual purpose. They are not only analyzed as standalone entities but also provide crucial contextual information by linking to files, domains, and other indicators observed at that specific web location.

dashboardQueries.name: 6fec155f-e50c-491c-b8fb-845f92c100e8
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "FILE"
$date = timestamp.get_timestamp(cast.as_int(graph.additional.fields["last_analysis_date"]))
$md5 = graph.entity.file.md5
$file = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
match:
    $md5,$date,$file
order:
    $date desc
limit:
    10000

dashboardQueries.name: 9cf0234e-dddd-44fb-af72-598900eb4c1c
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.threat.severity = "MEDIUM"
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
outcome:
    $count=count_distinct($entityid)

dashboardQueries.name: 5e81fcf0-6c8e-4786-b864-c8620ed47ce7
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
$threatlistid = graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"]
$date=timestamp.get_timestamp(graph.metadata.interval.start_time.seconds, "%Y-%m-%d")
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
match:
    $date, $threatlistid
outcome:
    $count=count_distinct($entityid)
order:
    $count desc
limit:
    10000

dashboardQueries.name: b9a19b87-5ba9-44fb-a52f-0b3f1a3dd486
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "URL"
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
$category = graph.entity.url_metadata.categories
$category != ""
match:
    $category
outcome:
    $count = count_distinct($entityid)
order:
    $count desc
limit:
    10000

dashboardQueries.name: 476a7198-d501-4a46-8c62-2102aa3f7e2e
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] != ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "URL"
$reponsecode = if(graph.entity.url_metadata.last_http_response_code!=0, cast.as_string(graph.entity.url_metadata.last_http_response_code), "-")
$date = timestamp.get_timestamp(cast.as_int(graph.additional.fields["last_analysis_date"]))
$url = graph.entity.url
match:
    $url,$reponsecode,$date
order:
    $date desc
limit:
    10000
